function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < count' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < count' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < count' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < count' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 5:  <statement> char* names[] = { </statement> is reached, the statement line 6:  <statement> "Alan", "Frank", </statement> has already been reached?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 11:  <statement> for (i = 0; i < count; ++i) { </statement> is reached, the statement line 7:  <statement> "Mary", "John", "Lisa" </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable cur_age at line 15: <statement>   int *cur_age = ages; </statement> depend on the value of variable ages at line 4: <statement>   int ages[] = { 23, 43, 12, 89, 2 }; </statement>?
Does the value of variable i at line 10: <statement>   int i = 0; </statement> depend on the value of variable argc at line 2: <statement> int main(int argc, char* argv[]) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'ages' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'argc' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'printf("---\n");' be considered dead code?
Is this statement not dead: “printf("%s is %d years old.\n", *(cur_name + i), *(cur_age + i));”?
